zh.lifeSummary: 當Gerard de L'Isle II 1st Baron Lisle於1304年在英國波克夏金斯頓 李爾受洗時，他的父親Warin de L'Isle41歲，母親Alice de Monfort34歲。他於大約1332年與Eleanor FitzAlan在英國格盧斯特斯爾沃頓 武安德 艾其結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1360年6月9日在英國斯塔福爾史道威享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gerard de L'Isle II 1st Baron Lisle于1304年在英国波克夏金斯頓 李爾受洗时，他的父亲Warin de L'Isle41岁，母亲Alice de Monfort34岁。他于大约1332年与Eleanor FitzAlan在英国格盧斯特斯爾沃頓 武安德 艾其结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1360年6月9日在英国斯塔福爾史道威享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: L'Isle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northumberland, Durham, and Yorkshire): variant of   Lyle   and a cognate of 2 below.
French: habitational name from L'Isle or Lisle, names of several places in various parts of France, named with Old French isle ‘island’ (from Latin insula). Compare Delisle .

variants.name: Lyle
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin): topographic or habitational name from Anglo-Norman French del isle ‘from the island’ (from Latin de insula), denoting someone who lived on an island or who came from a place in France named Ile, Isle, or L'Isle. The surname may also in some cases be from Lille (Nord). Compare Lisle and Iles .

variants.name: Lile
meanings: English and French: variant of   Lisle  .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + hard ‘brave, hardy, strong’. In the later Middle Ages this was a more common name than Gerald , with which it was sometimes confused. Nowadays it is less common, surviving mainly among Roman Catholics, in honour of the many saints of this name.

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: Variant spelling of   Gerard  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Jerrard
meanings: Rare variant spelling of   Gerard  , probably influenced by the form of the modern surname, if not a transferred use.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Gerard de L'Isle II 1st Baron Lisle was christened in 1304, in Kingston Lisle, Berkshire, England, his father, Warin de L'Isle, was 41 and his mother, Alice de Monfort, was 34. He married Eleanor FitzAlan about 1332, in Wotton under Edge, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 9 June 1360, in Stowe, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gerard de L'Isle II 1st Baron Lisle a été baptisé en 1304, à Kingston Lisle, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Warin de L'Isle, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Alice de Monfort, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor FitzAlan environ 1332, à Wotton under Edge, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 9 juin 1360, à Stowe, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gerard de L'Isle II 1st Baron Lisle è stato battezzato nel 1304 nel Kingston Lisle, Berkshire, England, il suo padre, Warin de L'Isle, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Alice de Monfort, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor FitzAlan circa 1332, nel Wotton under Edge, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 9 giugno 1360, nel Stowe, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gerard de L'Isle II 1st Baron Lisleが1304年にイギリスバークシュアイアキングストン リスルで洗礼を受けた時，父Warin de L'Isleは41歳，母Alice de Monfortは34歳であった。約1332年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアウオットオン ウンダー エッジでEleanor FitzAlanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1360年6月9日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアストウで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gerard de L'Isle II 1st Baron Lisle(이)가 1304년 영국 버크실르 킨그스턴 리슬일에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Warin de L'Isle(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Monfort(은)는 34세였다. 그는 대략 1332년 영국 그루스스터실르 보턴 운더 에지에서 Eleanor FitzAlan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1360년 6월 9일 영국 스태퍼드실르 스타우에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gerard de L'Isle II 1st Baron Lisle foi batizado em 1304, em Kingston Lisle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Warin de L'Isle, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Monfort, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor FitzAlan aproximadamente 1332, em Wotton under Edge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 9 de junho de 1360, em Stowe, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gerard de L'Isle II 1st Baron Lisle был крещен в 1304 г. в местности кингстон лисл, биркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Warin de L'Isle было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Alice de Monfort, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor FitzAlan около 1332 г. в местности воттон ундир эдж, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 9 июня 1360 г. в местности стоу, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.

Se casó con Eleanor FitzAlan aproximadamente 1332, en Wotton under Edge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
